reviews.review_description:
Rager is a super fun twist on the usual VR rhythm games. Instead of watching notes fly in from across the room, this game throws enemies right up in your face, so you’ve gotta rely on quick reactions instead of memorizing patterns. It definitely keeps you on edge in a good way.

One thing I really liked is how each level gives you different weapons to mess around with. You’re always adjusting your style depending on what you’re using. It’s a nice change from games where you just stick to the same gear the whole time.

The music is great too—high-energy tracks that match the action perfectly and really pull you into the chaos.

If you want a VR rhythm game that’s a bit more intense and hands-on than the usual “see the note, hit the note” formula, Rager is totally worth checking out. It’s fast, it’s stylish, and it keeps you hooked.

reviews.review_title: Good Demo, won't be getting full game
reviews.review_description:
Enjoyed the demo of Rager.  Or the version I have anyways.  Won't be buying the full game though (despite it being on my wish-list for a good while).  It seems the finished game is now 360, which is cool and all.  But, without MR, I just don't have room for that (I know from experience I'll end up breaking things - probably my hands/neck).  The 180 OG demo was perfect.  But yeah, gonna have to skip this now... :/ 

*Might take another look if I notice it gets MR at some point.

developer_response.body: Players talk and we listen — Mixed Reality is now supported!

reviews.review_description:
Hello, I collaborate with a Telegram channel about VR in Russia, could you answer a couple of questions:
How did you come up with the idea for such a game?
Will you rely on the mechanics of Beat Saber?
Do you expect to replicate the success of Beat Saber (it already looks quite good)?
What price do you plan to set for the full version of the game?
Are you considering making the game free and earning from paid DLC?
Will you be opposed to the development of mods for your game?

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the review! Of course, hit me up by email.

reviews.review_description: This game works for what I feel like it's trying to achieve. Musics good, the different modes, the only thing I can see being a small problem is that sometimes with double swings on the mace section it sometimes doesn't react aka it ignores the input even though I swinged both in the right direction...but is that because my maces were too close together? Regardless fun game and I hope soon this fall the full game releases cause I'm gonna get it.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review! I think that we've already fixed an issue you're describing. We've also recently released Early Access version if you want to check it out!

reviews.review_description: I enjoy rhythm games and this one takes it to the next level! It is SO much fun to slay monsters instead of boring targets. Right now the demo only has two weapons to try but according to the trailer there are more types of weapons to come in the full version. I’m excited for this to launch and definitely will be buying it.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review and feedback!

reviews.review_description: Just watched a Tron: Ares trailer and it totally reminded me of this super cool app!!! Think Beat Saber but instead of shapes you fight enemies and it is so darn cool! The visuals are awesome as is the music...and this app makes for an amazing workout! Cannot wait to have the full version!!! And one note in regards to the voice used to train you, PLEASE incorporate that voice more...maybe like some back story at the beginning to tell us who the enemies are and why we need to fight them, or maybe in a break have it let us know what is coming next...! That voice really sets the tone for Rager and for whatever reason i cannot get enough of it! A+++

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for awesome review and feedback!

reviews.review_description: Demo is very good, gives a nice preview of what’s in early access.
It’s good, but it never works even tho you slash in the right direction and it outright glitches, crashes, and has a crap ton of bugs

developer_response.body: Really sorry you didn't like the experience. The version you tried was very outdated, we've managed to progress a lot since that time and today released completely reworked demo version, so I really recommend to give it a try:)

reviews.review_description: Awesome new update today!  Wishlisting now for the full release!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review! Really appreciate that!

reviews.review_description: Fun, simple to learn, hard to master. And it has a campaign

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your feedback!:)

reviews.review_title: 62 and this will keep you in shape !
reviews.review_description: Fast and quick .. tutorial was decent and haven't gotten into real game play ! Just got out of Acer shooting range and saw this on horizon feed ! Said get so I got !!  It will probably kick my 62 year old butt but looks fun !! Letter rip tarter chip!! Lol

developer_response.body:
Edit: Hey! We've just released a huge update for the demo version!

Thank you very much for your feedback and review! We are working hard to bring the full game to the early access this year and we hope that you're gonna like it, because it'll be much improved!

reviews.review_title: Can't wait for the full game
reviews.review_description:
Cool style, cool visuals, cool gameplay and a good workout.

My only minor nitpick was that I feel like the block from above should be a little higher, but maybe that's just me.  

Other than that, in the full game it would be really cool if you could be able to add your own songs, but I don't know how realistic a  request that is.

Regardless, very solid demo, I think the full game has the potential to be a classic, keep up the good work. **UPDATE**. Played some more, I found it really fun when playing on easy mode (I don't think this will work on harder difficulties) putting one controller down and playing with a controller extension grip, so it ends up like I'm holding one longsword instead of two. Once again, looking forward to this game!

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for your feedback! It's great to hear that you like this version which is quite outdated as we've progressed a lot during the last year in Meta Ignition program. We'll announce a release date for the full game very soon and also release an update for the demo version by end of this month, so you can get a little taste of what's to come. You can always find tha latest info on our socials or even better on our Discord server.

About your requests, in the full game will definitely be an option to offset strike indicators and we also plan to implement in-game level editor.

reviews.review_description: In general amazing tutorial and nice and quick gets you straight into game play

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review!

reviews.review_description: Just Wow. After This Demo I am Sold. I Need more.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your awesome review!

reviews.review_description: Nice demo, like the game. The available modes are too slow for me, would have been nice to try out the hardest mode. Not a fan of the mace weapons though, found myself just holding them up and waiting for the enemies to fall into them, maybe it's better in a harder mode? Like the music. Looking forward to the full release, hope it's soon

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review and feedback! There are many issues in this demo version including the one you pointed out. All these issues will be gone and basically every aspect of the game will be improved in the full version. The full version will be released this year. I recommend to join our Discord server or follow our socials for all the important information about the release, etc.

reviews.review_description: Truly incredible for a demo. It reminds me of Against, a PCVR exclusive, and I’ve really wanted a game like that on the Quest system.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review and feedback! Really glad that you already like the demo version given the fact that full version will be much better!

reviews.review_title: Great game except for one annoying thing
reviews.review_description:
This is the game I've been looking for. Precise sword combat. Really fun.
The problem is that the orange ball that floats around is incredibly distracting. It's the brightest thing on the screen and breaks the immersion.  A setting to turn that off would make the game perfect.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review and feedback! You are right, lots of things in the demo version are not solved ideally, but definitelly will be in the full version. We are working hard to get RAGER to the whole new level, so hopefully you will like it!

reviews.review_description:
I don’t like the music I don’t like the way they tell us how to cut people I rather there be a multiplayer than the demo, but it’s an OK game 
￼

developer_response.body:
Edit: Hey! We've just released a huge update for the demo version!

Thank you for your review and feedback! This demo version is just a small showcase, the full version which will be released in 2025 will be much improved in every aspect and will definitely include multiplayer.

reviews.review_description: unlimited mode where it gets harder and faster over time(with a global leaderboard)

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review and suggestion! This game mode will be included in the full version for sure!

reviews.review_title: Solid game but needs a few tweaks
reviews.review_description:
At first when I downloaded this demo, I wasn't expecting much but it got me already with the tutorial. And the gameplay is fantastic, giving you a bit of a workout without it actually feeling like you're working out. 

The only thing I would like to say is that, it feels like the timing for the slash action occurs a bit too early. So most of the time I have to lean forward a lot to slash which makes me lose my balance a bit. The block and dodge action don't have that so it's a bit jarring sometimes.

But anyway there is definitely a solid game here. Hopefully they add the ability for custom songs and so on because that would be nice also

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review and feedback! Really appreciate that! In regards to timing, try to time your slash exactly when "both arrows" meet in the middle. You can also join our Discord where we can talk about your issue in a more detail.

reviews.review_title: Nice little demo with lots of promise.
reviews.review_description:
Hopefully this leads to a leveling up premise with boss battles after each level . 

The block , slash , duck premise also give a little workout . A rhythmic approach could also add to the immersion along with multiplayers for stringing block /slash /duck’s without error . 

The developers have a nice thing here , hopefully they put some substance in this .

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review and suggestion! Full version will have several game modes and we are exploring boss battle feature for "Campaign" game mode. Multiplayer is also on our road map.

reviews.review_description: I read a review describing this as "pistol whipped with swords" and that's pretty spot on! Good clear interactions, good tutorial and just a bunch of fun. Looking forward to seeing the full game in the meta store.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much!

reviews.review_title: Looks good so far. More, please!
reviews.review_description:
As others have mentioned, this is essentially Beat Saber meets Until You Fall. It's pretty dang polished for a demo. Nice balance of dodging, blocking and slashing, coming from different angles. The only problem I've encountered so far is hit register, which seems to be off quite a bit at times. It's unclear whether some attacks are supposed to be L or R sword-specific, or not.

I'd love to see: 
- Ranged enemies whose projectiles you can deflect back toward them
- Different arenas/environments
- Shorter times between "beats" or actions

This is well on its way. I can't wait to see more!

l i n k t r . e e
/
q u a c k b i r t h

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for review and suggestions! There are still things which will be polished including those you mentioned. Also for any additional suggestions or feedback you can join our Discord server.

reviews.review_description: Fast paced, quick thinking, it's pretty much just a free beat Saber. "Same same, but different"

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review! Unlike other rhythm games RAGER is oriented on melee combat. In this demo version you can play with swords and maces, but in full release will be many more weapons. Also goal of this game is to learn to play it without "Strike Indicators" which immerses you to the combat even more.

reviews.review_description: Only played demo but impressions are pretty good. It's  very satisfying and polished and def giving off Pistol Whip vibes (only swap for swords or maces) so that's all you need to know

developer_response.body: Thank you for review!

reviews.review_description: Like the title says...

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review! This is just a beginning, we are working hard to get it to the next level!

reviews.review_description:
Very cool, stylish and fun! The slash/block/dodge moves feel great and the soundtrack fits really well. Great work!

See some video gameplay here (remove the space before 'be' in the URL):

https://youtu. be/LII_u4PoZuA?si=XEj1wL1Ko_bj0NgI

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review!

reviews.review_title: The sword game I've been waiting for
reviews.review_description: Count me as your buyer.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review and for considering buying the full version of the game! The full version is in development, but it will take a little more time to release because we are basically improving every aspect of the game and want to polish it to the max. I recommend joining our Discord server or following our socials to stay up to date on the release.

reviews.review_description:
It's fun, and so good to see different visuals and music styles on the rhythm game.
please add movement: LEAP (reference: AGAINST)

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for review and suggestion! We will definitely explore this feature for future mods!

reviews.review_description:
I had a really great time with this demo. Took off a star just because of the limited options, environment, soundtrack etc but that's the expected with a demo. 
When is the full game going to be available?

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your feedback and review! The full game will include much more options of course and will be released this year. We are going to announce the release date this March.

reviews.review_description: I'm a huge fan of rhythm games and I really though this was super fun. I'm still working on the timing of when to block. It's like way more intense than beat saber and I absolutely loved it. Was totally sweating after a couple rounds.  It's a little hard to track where the phantom things are going to come from but I think it's based on the yellow orb....the problem is you don't see the yellow orb once you have a bunch of phantom guys rushing you lol. So fun and look forward to the full game. Definitely something I'll buy.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review! Yes, enemies are coming from the yellow orb which is a portal. Anyway, a full version of the game will be more polished overally, so basically everything will be improved.
